extract.description: Files and folders to extract from the downloaded mod zip

source.description: Path of the source file or folder within the downloaded mod zip

destination.description: Supports replacement tokens. Destination path to extract this file or folder to. If source was a folder, this must be a folder, if file, file.

write.description: Text files to write when installing the mod.

source.description: Relative path of the source file within this mod's folder in the mod-db repo. The contents of the file may contain replacement tokens.

destination.description: Supports replacement tokens. Destination path to write this file to.

path.description: Path relative to the mod folder, pointing to the executable/script to run

args.description: List of arguments to pass to the command. Tokens in the format of {{TOKEN}} get replaced with values from the game: {{ExecutableName}}, {{GameJson}}, {{ExecutablePath}}, {{StartCommand}}, {{StartCommandArgs}}, {{RunnablePath}}, {{LocalModsPath}}, {{RoamingAppData}}, {{ExecutableNameWithoutExtension}}

wineEnvironment.description: Environment variables to set when running the command via Wine. Tokens in the format of {{TOKEN}} get replaced with values from the game: {{ExecutableName}}, {{GameJson}}, {{ExecutablePath}}, {{StartCommand}}, {{StartCommandArgs}}, {{RunnablePath}}, {{LocalModsPath}}, {{RoamingAppData}}, {{ExecutableNameWithoutExtension}}

getConfig.description: Information used to find local mod configs, and also for downloading configs from the database.

destinationPath.description: Path in the user's PC where the config is installed to. This path can be relative to something, depending on the mod loader.

destinationType.description: If file, the downloaded config will be placed directly in destinationPath. If folder, the downloaded file must be a zip, and gets extracted to the destination path, which must be a folder.

modIdOverride.description: If set, overrides the mod ID used for finding config files. Useful when multiple mods use the same configs.

getModFolderForGame.description: How to get the folder that opens when pressing the 'Open Mod Folder' button.

path.description: Supports replacement tokens. This is the folder path that opens.
